member.office_address: 1027 Longworth House Office Building Washington DC 20515-1102

form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Please use this form to submit any programmatic, bill language, and report language requests for the Fiscal Year 2027 appropriations bills. If you have multiple requests, please fill out a separate form for each request. Bill or report language requests linked to any programmatic request should be submitted separately as well. A different submission form for Community Project Funding (CPF) requests can be found at https://airtable.com/appaq1qZxNQjbhQyO/shrBbANHA1bAn3wPU. Additional submissions may be accepted on a rolling basis pending official announcement of guidelines for Fiscal Year 2027 from the House Appropriations Committee. Please visit the House Appropriations Committee's website (https://appropriations.house.gov/) for additional information. If you have any questions or concerns, please contact Nick Luna (Legislative Director) at Nick.Luna@mail.house.gov or 202-225-4906.

top_grants.recipient_name: California Association for Research in Astronomy
top_grants.description: This proposal to NASA by the California Association for Research in Astronomy (CARA) dba W. M. Keck Observatory (WMKO) is for renewal of the cooperative agreement award for support of NASA missions and NASA scientific Research at wmko  which operates the world S two (2) largest steerable optical and infrared telescopes on the summit of maunakea on the island of hawaii. NASA utilizes one-sixth of the annual operation capability of wmko  having invested one-sixth of the capital costs and one-sixth of the operations costs since circa 1996. There has been a wealth of scientific results coming from the dynamic scientific relationship between NASA missions such as Hubble Space Telescope (HST)  Spitzer  Kepler  k2  Chandra  herschel  wise  galex  Voyager  galileo  deep impact  Cassini  new horizons  and the Keck Observatory. We propose here to extend that productive relationship to future NASA missions; JWST  TESS  WFIRST  euclid  Mars 2020  and other midex  smex  discovery  and new frontiers missions. The link between NASA and wmko is powerful. NASA S Space based discoveries inform and are informed by the findings of its ground based partner  the W. M. Keck Observatory. NASA related projects are proposed by the us community to nexsci and are awarded observing time provided by NASA. This has proven productive in the past. A prime example is when Kepler discovered objects transiting stars  wmko undertook key observations that established that the candidate exosolar planets were indeed valid exosolar planets  and measured their masses  leading to the achievement of one of NASA S level 1 science goals: making a census of planetary systems down to the level of earth-sized planets. When Spitzer and hst both detected an object in the distant universe  wmko delivered the exact redshift of 8.68  well within the region that was thought to be the dark age before reionization. The Research program enabled by the NASA/wmko partnership maps directly to the astrophysical and planetary priorities of NASA: the origin and evolution of the universe  the processes for life on other planets  the processes of formation of stars  galaxies  and planets  and the content  origin  and evolution of the solar system and the potential for life elsewhere. The scope of the astrophysics and planetary science is breathtaking  ranging from the birth of the universe and the development of stars and galaxies over cosmic time  to the search for life on planets around other stars  to the formation  evolution  and possibility of life on other planets in our own solar system. The two 10-meter primary apertures  diverse instrument suite including adaptive optics and the superb location on maunakea make wmko an extraordinarily powerful and unique facility for addressing the Research areas described above. Additionally  wmko can react quickly to NASA mission needs  rearranging nights on short notice. The track record of discoveries by scientists using the wmko in conjunction with NASA missions over the last two (2) decades gives strong testimony to that case. The program corresponding to NASA S in-guide funding amount provides NASA one-sixth of wmko operations support and  in collaboration with the NASA exoplanet science institute (NEXSCI)  wmko will continue its Keck Observatory Archive (KOA) activities. In addition  we submit here an over-guide that allows for the development of data reduction pipelines (DRPS) over a period of five (5) years  to speed the reduction of new data with improved tools and to access the information inherent in 25 years of  the Keck sky  easily and efficiently for all users. This proposal consolidates all direct NASA support for wmko into one award.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Supporting ground-based validation of NASA space telescope discoveries.
